description_long: This barn features a classic gable roof structure with exposed wooden rafters and beams, providing a skeletal view of its construction. The roof is partially open, revealing the internal truss system and allowing light to filter through the gaps. The exterior walls are constructed from light gray brickwork, giving the building a sturdy and utilitarian appearance. There are no visible doors or windows, emphasizing the unfinished or under-construction state of the barn. The overall form is rectangular with a symmetrical roof pitch, typical of agricultural outbuildings. The structure is spacious and designed to accommodate large equipment, livestock, or storage needs.
description: A rectangular barn with a gable roof, exposed wooden rafters, and light gray brick walls, lacking visible doors or windows.
description_view_0: End view of the barn showing the triangular gable roof and full brick wall beneath, with visible roof trusses.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open roof structure, exposed rafters, and the long brick wall with no openings.
description_view_2: Opposite end view mirroring view 0, showing the gable roof and brick wall, with visible internal trusses.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, displaying the open roof framework and uninterrupted brick wall.
